channel.title: David Sheeren on David Sheeren
channel.description: Recent content in David Sheeren on David Sheeren

item.title: Hosting the SAGEO'2026 Conference in Toulouse!
item.description: Save the date! The SAGEO’2026 Conference will take place from November 17 to 20, 2026, in Toulouse, at INP-AgroToulouse. This conference is organized by the DYNAFOR, LISST, CESBIO, TRACES, and ODR research laboratories. Held under the auspices of the CNRS MAGIS Research Network (GdR MAGIS), the SAGEO Conference is a major annual french event in the field of spatial data science. It provides a platform for the dissemination and discussion of recent scientific advances in geomatics, spatial modeling, and spatial analysis. The conference will also offers a unique opportunity for researchers from diverse disciplines to meet and exchange ideas.

item.title: A review of the index of biodiversity potential (IBP) as a tool for biodiversity evaluation in forest ecosystems
Habitat quality assessment of temperate forest ecosystems: an airborne LiDAR-based approach to predict the Index of Biodiversity Potential (IBP) at large scale
SilviLaser'2025 Conference in Québec City!
item.description: Save the date! The next SilviLaser conference SilviLaser’2025 will be at Québec City, QC (Canada) from September 29 to October 3. The symposium is organized by the University of Laval, faculty of Forestry, Geography and Geomatics. Manon Collard will present our first results of modeling the ecological quality of forests using LiDAR data (see the abstract of the talk).

item.title: Improved central place foraging modeling: importance of landscape context and modeling strategies in the case of a French sweat bee
Submonthly Assessment of Temperate Forest Clear-Cuts in Mainland France
End-to-end simulations to optimize imaging spectroscopy mission requirements for seven scientific applications
A new method for quantifying treeline-ecotone change based on multiple spatial pattern dimensions
BIODIVERSITY: Une nouvelle mission spatiale pour le suivi des écosystèmes à une échelle fine
Sentinel-2 Poplar Index for Operational Mapping of Poplar Plantations over Large Areas
How far is enough? Prediction of the scale of effect for wild bees
Individual Tree Crown Delineation Method Based on Multi-Criteria Graph Using Geometric and Spectral Information: Application to Several Temperate Forest Sites
Farming intensity indirectly reduces crop yield through negative effects on agrobiodiversity and key ecological functions
Award for outstanding partnership and collaboration
item.description: Really happy to receive this prize with Yousra Hamrouni (my PhD student - now graduated!) during the third edition of the Trophées de la Valorisation de la Recherche organized by Toulouse Tech Transfer for outstanding partnership and collaboration with CNP (Conseil National du Peuplier) and CNPF-IDF (Centre National de la Propriété Forestière). A short video is available here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=98Vs_Qs2Ix0

item.title: THEIA Workshop on forest remote sensing
item.description: The french THEIA Data and Services Centre for EO organized on 11 October 2021 a one-day workshop on forest remote sensing. More than a hundred participants attended this workshop with various presentations and discussions related to tree species classification, biomass estimation, deforestation monitoring in tropical regions, mapping health status, detecting forest fires… The presenations can be found here https://www.theia-land.fr/foret/foret2021/. You will find my presentation on the detection of poplar plantations using Sentinel-2 time series (PhD findings of Y. Hamrouni).
